question: Is the painting containing beige tones and a curved arch line (originally in the center) now located at the leftmost position on the wall?
Is the painting with lighter grey and beige blocks (originally on the right) now positioned in the center of the arrangement?
Is the painting with heavy black abstract shapes (originally on the left) now located at the far right position on the wall?
Does the internal artwork content of each painting remain unchanged (no morphing or composition alteration) despite the position swap?
Are there exactly three framed paintings on the wall (no duplicates, missing frames, or partial fragments)?
Are all three paintings aligned horizontally at the same height (top and bottom edges aligned) without vertical drift?
Do the relocated paintings cast realistic drop shadows on the wall consistent with the directional sunlight coming from the right window?
Is the wall surface behind and around the paintings free from 'ghosting' artifacts, smudges, or lighter/darker patches where the frames used to be?
Is the grey sofa below the paintings preserved completely (shape, texture, and position) without warping near the painting area?
Are the edited paintings and the surrounding wall area as sharp and detailed as the rest of the image (no localized blur)?
